description: Geo Round 2: score economy with clock-as-pacer, hacker-cohesive round UI + end-of-round breakdown, and the retention layer (trophy map, heatmap, streak freezes, local-date rollover, save code)

userStories.title: Clock becomes a pure pacer (no time mutations)
userStories.description: As a player, I want the 60-second clock to only pace the round so that penalties and bonuses never steal or add time.
acceptanceCriteria: Remove all time-mutating rules from the round: wrong-guess time penalty, correct-streak +2s bonus, and zoom time costs (ZOOM_PENALTY_SECONDS / WORLD_REVEAL_SURCHARGE_SECONDS) no longer change remainingSeconds
Clock counts 60 -> 0 at 1s/tick regardless of any player action
Reaching 0:00 no longer ends or fails the round (transitional behavior until US-003: round simply continues; hints are fully drawn at 0)
RoundCore reducer tests updated to assert time is never mutated by guess or zoom events
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation for the whole economy. Files: src/lib/game/clock.ts, src/lib/game/round.ts, src/lib/game/zoom.ts. Do not delete old economy tests wholesale - migrate each rule's test to its new score-based counterpart in US-002/US-004.

userStories.title: Event-sourced score with combo multiplier in RoundCore
userStories.description: As a player, I want letters to earn and cost points with a visible combo so that the score reflects how I'm playing, not just the clock.
acceptanceCriteria: Correct letter awards +100 x combo multiplier; combo steps x1 -> x1.5 -> x2 (capped at x2) with each consecutive correct letter
Wrong letter resets combo to x1 and deducts points scaled by the target's unique-letter count: <=5 unique -> -200, 6-9 -> -150, 10+ -> -100
All values are exported named constants in one module (tunable)
Score floor is 0 (never displays negative)
RoundCore emits a score-event record per event (type, delta, resulting multiplier) consumable by the UI
Reducer unit tests cover: combo build, combo cap, combo reset, each wrong-letter tier, score floor
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Replaces score-as-f(clock) in src/lib/game/score.ts. RoundCore (src/lib/game/round.ts) is a pure reducer - keep it that way.

userStories.title: Soft zero: lockout mode, outcome tiers, time bonus
userStories.description: As a player, I want to keep solving after 0:00 with limited attempts so that running out of time creates tension instead of instant failure.
acceptanceCriteria: At 0:00 the round enters lockout mode: guessing stays enabled, 5-attempt wrong-guess budget (exported constant), each wrong letter burns one
Round outcomes are exactly: solved (before 0:00), solved_late (after 0:00), locked_out (budget exhausted), gave_up
Time bonus = remainingSeconds x 10 awarded only on 'solved' (0 remaining or late solve -> no time bonus)
solved and solved_late both count as solves; locked_out and gave_up yield final score 0
Give up button produces gave_up in both phases
Reducer tests cover: transition into lockout, budget decrement, locked_out at 0 budget, solved_late scoring without time bonus, gave_up zeroing
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Lockout UI treatment (pips, 'SYSTEM LOCKOUT IN 5 ATTEMPTS') lands in US-006/US-007; this story is reducer + minimal wiring so the round is playable end-to-end.

userStories.title: Zoom penalties move to score: -10/step, -100 cap, pay-once
userStories.description: As a player, I want zooming out to cost a bounded number of points so that exploring the map is a priced hint, never a round-ender.
acceptanceCriteria: Each newly-crossed zoom-out step deducts 10 points; total zoom deductions hard-capped at 100 per round (both exported constants)
Pay-once: re-crossing an already-paid step never re-charges; zooming back in never refunds
Reaching max zoom (world reveal) charges no time and nothing beyond the -100 cap
Zoom remains free after the round ends (any outcome)
Zoom score events flow through the US-002 event record for UI popups
Tests cover: step charge, cap enforcement, pay-once on re-cross, post-round free zoom
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/lib/game/zoom.ts keeps step detection; the charge becomes a RoundCore score event. Deletes the 'zoom out to death' failure mode by construction.

userStories.title: Live score readout with multiplier and event popups
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see my score, combo multiplier, and each +/- event as it happens so that I stay engaged while solving.
acceptanceCriteria: Score readout shows running score and current combo multiplier (multiplier visually distinct when above x1)
Each score event (letter gain, wrong-letter loss, zoom cost, time bonus) fires a transient popup (+200 / -150 style) near the readout; no popup for ordinary ticks
Popups animate in/out within ~1.5s and never stack illegibly (concurrent events queue or offset)
Green for positive events, red permitted for negative events, monochrome otherwise (theme guardrails)
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill (screenshot: readout at x2 combo with a visible popup, via dev server ?date= override)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser, add-motion, build-ui. Replaces the static top-right score. Placement may be refined again by US-006 (hierarchy) - keep the component self-contained.

userStories.title: Round-surface hierarchy rework
userStories.description: As a player, I want the score and question to be the loudest elements after the map so that my attention lands where the game is.
acceptanceCriteria: Score readout and question have greater visual prominence (size/position/contrast) than zoom controls; zoom controls recede (smaller/lower-contrast) but stay usable
Question and answer slots sit in a deliberate relationship with the keyboard (no orphaned question floating between unrelated elements)
Lockout mode surfaces the 5-attempt pips with the label 'SYSTEM LOCKOUT IN 5 ATTEMPTS' (or agent-refined intrusion-log phrasing) when the clock hits 0:00
The 'Geo #N - Solved/Failed' outcome strip and the floating Copy button are REMOVED from the round surface (they return inside the end screen, US-012/US-013)
Minimal monochrome identity preserved: black field, Geist Mono, dot-matrix clock untouched
No horizontal overflow at 390x844 (use per-element getBoundingClientRect, not body.scrollWidth)
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill (screenshots: desktop 1280px and mobile 390px, mid-round and lockout mode)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser, build-ui, audit-ui. Theme guardrails in prd-geo-round-2.md 'Solution > M2' are binding: no green-rain, no CRT/scanline filters, single green accent.

userStories.title: Guess-feedback animations (letter placement + reject)
userStories.description: As a player, I want letters to land in their slots with a satisfying motion and wrong guesses to visibly reject so that each guess feels like an event.
acceptanceCriteria: Correct letters animate into their answer slots (slide/decode-style, consistent with the scramble language already used by neighbor labels)
Wrong guesses produce a visible reject animation (e.g. shake/flash on the guessed key or slot row) distinct from the correct animation
Multi-slot letters (e.g. two E's) animate all their slots
Animations respect prefers-reduced-motion (reduced or disabled)
Physical keyboard and on-screen keyboard trigger identical feedback
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill (correct guess and wrong guess captured)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: add-motion, automate-browser. Framer Motion already in the stack - prefer it before adding any animation dependency.

userStories.title: Retune mouse-wheel zoom responsiveness
userStories.description: As a player, I want scroll-wheel zoom to feel responsive so that exploring the map isn't sluggish.
acceptanceCriteria: Wheel delta -> zoom change mapping retuned so a normal wheel/trackpad gesture produces a clearly visible zoom change (document the old vs new mapping constant in the diff)
Pinch/trackpad zoom and +/- buttons remain functional and proportionate
Zoom step-crossing detection (US-004 charges) still fires exactly once per step at the new sensitivity (test or manual verification note)
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser. Pure tuning story - no design decisions.

userStories.title: Ocean hatch: subtle flow + zoom-dependent density
userStories.description: As a player, I want the ocean to read as water so that the world map feels alive without stealing attention.
acceptanceCriteria: Diagonal hatch identity preserved (no texture/image replacement)
Hatch has a subtle continuous motion (drift/shimmer); subtle means the target outline remains the dominant moving element (agent judgment, screenshot-defended)
Hatch density/opacity scales with zoom so slight zoom-out no longer reads as overwhelming line noise (compare screenshots at default zoom, ~2 steps out, and full world)
Animation respects prefers-reduced-motion (static fallback)
No visible frame drops while the outline draw-in runs concurrently (note findings; WorldMapLayer is unmemoized and 3x-tiled - do not regress the wrap)
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill (three zoom-level screenshots)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: add-motion, automate-browser. Perf caution: prefer CSS/SVG pattern animation over per-frame React re-render.

userStories.title: fun_fact field in the country dataset
userStories.description: As a player, I want each country to have a lighter fun fact alongside the educational question so that the round has a moment of delight.
acceptanceCriteria: Country dataset schema gains fun_fact: string; generation/merge scripts (scripts/generate-countries-geo.mjs / merge-country-metadata.mjs) carry it through

All countries in the shipped dataset have a non-empty fun_fact (LLM-generated, one or two sentences, surprising/light tone - e.g. 'This country has the largest population without a single McDonald's')
Facts must not contain the country's name or demonym (spoiler guard - enforce with a script check in the generation pipeline)
A review manifest (e.g. facts flagged needs-review: true until human-reviewed) exists so the human pass is trackable
npm run gen:countries pipeline runs clean
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same pipeline + review bar as existing trivia. Hint-strength grading is an open question in the PRD - the spoiler guard (no name/demonym) is the enforceable floor.

userStories.title: Fun-fact line beneath the trivia question
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see the fun fact under the question so that every round teaches me something delightful.
acceptanceCriteria: fun_fact renders beneath the trivia question, visually subordinate (smaller/dimmer)
Visible for the whole round and after round end (same lifetime as the trivia question)
Missing/empty fun_fact renders nothing (no empty container)
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser. PRD open question: reveal timing (start vs mid-round) - shipping start-visible as the default; make the reveal timing trivially changeable (single constant/prop).

userStories.title: End screen Act 1: itemized score breakdown
userStories.description: As a player, I want a Street-Fighter-style score recap when the round ends so that my result tells a story.
acceptanceCriteria: On round end (any outcome) a breakdown presents line items sequentially/animated: letters earned (with combo context), mistakes, recon cost (zoom), time bonus, TOTAL
Line items use intrusion-log vocabulary (e.g. 'TRACE PENALTY', 'SPEED BONUS'); labels are agent-craftable within theme guardrails
Outcome and day number appear here (e.g. 'GEO #9 - ACCESS GRANTED' / late / locked-out / gave-up variants) - the old outcome strip must already be gone (US-006)
Line items sum exactly to the final score from RoundCore (assert in a test against a fixed reducer scenario)
Solved-late shows no time bonus line or an explicit zero line; locked_out/gave_up show TOTAL 0
Confetti still fires only on genuine solves (solved or solved_late), once
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill (screenshots: solved and locked_out breakdowns via ?date= override)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser, add-motion, polish-copy. The breakdown reads from US-002's score-event records - no re-derivation.

userStories.title: End screen Act 2 shell: share, copy, countdown to tomorrow
userStories.description: As a player, I want the end screen to hand me the share and point me at tomorrow so that finishing today plants the return trip.
acceptanceCriteria: After Act 1, an Act 2 section shows: share text preview, a Copy button, and a live countdown to the next local-midnight round
Share/copy exists ONLY here (round surface has none)
Share string covers all four outcomes: day number, result (time-remaining when solved / late / failed), spoiler-free guess-pattern row; country name+flag included only for solved and solved_late
Copy button writes the exact share string to the clipboard (verify via clipboard read in browser automation)
Current streak count displays (freeze/heatmap/map arrive in later stories - layout leaves room)
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill
Tests pass (share-string generation for all four outcomes)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser, polish-copy. Extends src/lib/share/. Lockout-mode outcome representation in the pattern row is an open question - pick a spoiler-safe encoding and document it in the diff.

userStories.title: Local-date rollover: clean flip from UTC
userStories.description: As a player, I want the new country to arrive at my local midnight so that the daily ritual fits my day.
acceptanceCriteria: Daily hash input becomes the device's local calendar date string (via the boot seam - src/lib/game/boot.ts); ?date= dev override still works
Day number = days since a fixed epoch of the LOCAL date, with the epoch chosen so today's number continues the current public sequence (document the chosen epoch in the diff)
Idempotent by date: a local date with a recorded result always shows that result (no replay); a date change while the app is open picks up the new day on next boot/visibility change
No UTC migration shim (accepted one-time discontinuity)
Tests: same local date -> same country; consecutive dates -> generally different countries; epoch math; ?date= override precedence
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: src/lib/game/dailyCountry.ts + boot.ts. Everything in M3 keys off local dates - this must land first.

userStories.title: Outcome ledger + trophy-map records in localStorage
userStories.description: As a player, I want every day's result and every solved country remembered so that my history can be shown and protected.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-day ledger keyed by local date: { outcome: solved | solved_late | failed, score, target } written at round end (failed = locked_out or gave_up; absent key = missed day)
Trophy-map record keyed by country code: { tier: in_time | late, date } written ONLY for solves (failures leave no map trace)
Existing streak read/write (src/lib/streak/) migrates to derive from the ledger (one-time migration of current streak fields; no data loss for current/best)
Storage schema carries a version field for the save code (US-019) to reference
Tests: ledger write per outcome, map write only on solves, streak derivation incl. gap = reset, migration from legacy keys
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Data model in prd-geo-round-2.md is illustrative - final field names are implementer's choice, but versioned. This is the source of truth for heatmap, freezes, map, and save code.

userStories.title: Streak freezes: earn, bank, auto-apply, kind messaging
userStories.description: As a player, I want a missed day to be coverable by an earned freeze so that one bad day doesn't erase my streak.
acceptanceCriteria: Earn 1 freeze per 5 consecutive solved days (solved_late counts); bank caps at 2; both exported constants
On visit after missed day(s): freezes auto-apply chronologically to bridge the gap where possible; applied days recorded as outcome 'frozen' in the ledger
Explicit message on next visit when a freeze was consumed ('A streak freeze covered <date>' phrasing agent-craftable)
If the gap exceeds banked freezes: streak resets, and the break message celebrates the ended run's record (never shame framing)
One-line rule copy visible where freezes are shown (Duolingo copy-explainer win)
Tests: earn cadence, bank cap, single/multi-day auto-apply, insufficient-freezes reset, frozen ledger entries
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: polish-copy (messaging). Freeze application is recomputed/applied at boot from the ledger - keep it deterministic so the save code round-trips.

userStories.title: Contribution heatmap (honest ledger view)
userStories.description: As a player, I want a GitHub-style calendar of my daily results so that my streak has a visible body.
acceptanceCriteria: Calendar grid of day cells from the ledger: bright green = solved, mid green = solved_late, distinct failed cell, empty = missed, distinct freeze cell
Failed and frozen cells are distinguishable by shape/glyph, not color alone (colorblind-safe)
End screen Act 2 embeds a compact recent window (~10-12 weeks); a stats view shows full history
Today's cell updates immediately at round end
Cell tooltip/tap shows date + outcome (+ score for solves)
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill (screenshot with a seeded ledger covering all five cell states)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser, build-ui. Seed test data through localStorage in the automation script to show all states. Grid aesthetic is deeply on-theme - keep it terminal-clean.

userStories.title: Trophy world map with tiered fills
userStories.description: As a player, I want solved countries to fill in on a world map so that a multi-year collection grows out of my daily plays.
acceptanceCriteria: End screen Act 2 renders the world map (existing WorldMapLayer at full zoom-out) with solved countries filled: bright = in_time, dim = late; unsolved/failed countries unfilled
Today's solve animates its fill when the end screen opens
Progress counter 'N/190' (or actual dataset count) displays with the map
Post-round free exploration unaffected
End screen open causes no visible jank (WorldMapLayer is unmemoized + 3x tiled - memoize or verify; note findings)
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill (screenshot with seeded map records showing both tiers)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser, add-motion. Strongest-evidence retention feature in the research - the fill moment should feel like the day's trophy.

userStories.title: Save code: export/import full player state
userStories.description: As a player, I want a copyable code that restores my streak, map, and history so that a browser wipe can't rob me.
acceptanceCriteria: Export produces a versioned string (e.g. v1.<base64url(json)>) encoding ledger, map, streak, and freeze state; import parses, validates version + shape, and restores
Round-trip test: export -> wipe localStorage -> import -> identical derived state (streak, freezes, map count, heatmap cells)
Import of a corrupt/unversioned string fails safely with a clear message and touches nothing
Import merges by taking, per local date, the better outcome when both sides have records (no silent clobber of a longer history)
Export/import UI lives in the stats area (copy button + paste field)
Verify in browser using automate-browser skill
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser. This codec becomes the future Supabase sync payload - keep it a pure src/lib/save/ module with no DOM dependencies.

userStories.title: Full-flow QA sweep across all four outcomes
userStories.description: As the maintainer, I want the complete round-to-return loop verified end-to-end so that competing implementations can be judged on evidence.
acceptanceCriteria: Scripted browser runs via ?date= override produce screenshot sets for: solved in-time, solved late (past 0:00), locked out, gave up - each covering round surface, lockout mode where applicable, Act 1 breakdown, Act 2
Desktop (1280px) and mobile (390px) sets; no horizontal overflow at 390px (getBoundingClientRect per element)
Share string copied and verified for each outcome (country revealed only on solves)
Multi-day sequence (3+ consecutive ?date= days incl. one deliberate miss with a banked freeze) shows correct streak, freeze consumption, heatmap cells, and map fills
npm run test -- --run fully green; npm run build succeeds
All screenshots saved to a review directory and listed in notes for comparison
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Suggested skills: automate-browser, audit-ui. Final gate story - this is the evidence package the branch competition is judged on.
